reason: Verified against the actual files: the change is sound and I cannot concretely refute it. (1) The prompt rewrite does NOT under-flag — it KEEPS the correct high bar (PII-to-undocumented-store / deletion-gap-misses-a-store / stale documented-or-indexed derived field) and ADDS a derived-field-desync high class plus a rubric carve-out; no severity or threshold is loosened. (2) No known-bad slips through: the only fixture removed is bad/untransformed-write, which is itself INVALID — its change.patch demands flagging verbatim-name storage (Item(name=name), strip/lower skipped) that the template's own src/framework_cli/template/.../repository.py create_item does verbatim-and-unflagged (item = Item(name=name)), i.e. a codebase-bar violation, and is out of the data-lineage domain (no PII sink, no deletion gap). All 3 baseline repeats (untransformed-write__r0/r1/r2) correctly returned [], so its recall contribution is 0 and it is the sole cause of recall 0.17 — the reviewers correctly abstained, confirming a fixture-grading bug not a prompt/threshold defect. (3) Not tuned against a dirty good fixture: good/normalized-write routes through create_item_normalized (strip+lower), is genuinely clean, scores [] on all 3 repeats, and is untouched by the change. (4) The genuine bad fixture bad/stale-derived-field (rename_item mutates item.name without recomputing the documented unique/indexed slug) is RETAINED and was already flagged medium/medium/high across repeats; the added high-pinned derived-field-desync class strengthens it. The only residual concerns are Phase-3 calibration, not correctness: the replacement is specified as a sketch (deletion-gap OR PII-to-undocumented-sink) rather than an authored patch, and raising recall_min 0.73->0.90 on a 2-bad-fixture corpus where stale-derived-field hit high only 1/3 at baseline is aggressive — but both are explicitly deferred to the Phase-3 re-derivation and do not make the shipped change wrong. DEFAULT-to-refuted overridden because verification was positive, not uncertain.
